rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT PICASO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Heirship
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/23/2022 at request of counsel. Examiner does not have the required courtesy copies or proposed order. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HATSUME DORNECKER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions Order To Reimburse Attorney Expenses And To Accept Verification From Distributees Under Japanese Law
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The attorney for the administrator of the will annexed requests reimbursement of $5,223.06 for expenses incurred by the attorney between January 2018 and June 2021. The balance of the estate cash on hand is $17,610.22, but two beneficiaries have received $8,000 x 2 less than the amounts ordered to be distributed. The order for final distribution has become final and conclusive as to the rights of all interested persons under Probate Code section 11605. The order has not been appealed or set aside by a motion under CCP section 473(b). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DELIA W. FOSTER 1995 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respondent Botto Law Group To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Delia Foster established the Delia W. Foster 1995 Trust on June 19, 1995, naming herself as trustee. The Trust was allegedly drafted by Respondent Gary Botto and/or Respondent law firm Botto Law Group. Botto was named successor trustee. Foster executed the Twelfth and final amendment to the Trust on March 30, 2015. The amendment was allegedly drafted by Respondent Botto and/or his law firm, and Botto was stilled named successor trustee. Foster allegedly named Botto her attorney in fact in 2015. Foster died on May 21, 2019. Petitioners are Foster's nephews and heirs at law. On August 27, 2020, they filed Petition for (1) Order Compelling Trust Accounting; (2) Order Compelling Accounting by Attorney in Fact; (3) Breach of Fiduciary Duty; (4) Professional Negligence; (5) Return of Trust Property (Prob. Code section 850); (6) Financial Elder Abuse; (7) Removal of Trustee; (8) Immediate Suspension of Trustee's Powers Pending Removal; and (9) Order Instruction Trustee to Refrain from Using Trust Assets to Defend, naming Botto and Botto Law Group among the Respondents. On February 10, 2022, Petitioners filed Motion to Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto to Provide Further Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One. Petitioners seek further responses to Special Interrogatories No. 6, 7, 47, 48, 49, 50, and Motion to Compel Respondent Botto Law Group to Provide Further Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One. Petitioners seek further responses to Special Interrogatories No. 79, 80, 81, 82. Also on February 10, 2022, Botto filed Motion for Protective Order, Request for Sanctions. The Motions to Compel are scheduled for hearing before the court on July 13, 2022. Botto's Motion for Protective Order is scheduled for hearing before the court on July 20, 2022. Motion to Compel Respondent Botto Law Group to Provide Further Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One as to Special Interrogatories No. 79, 80, 81, 82 is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Botto Law Group is ordered to provide further responses to Special Interrogatories No. 79, 80, 81, 82, limited in time to 2019 and 2020 and with any necessary redactions to preserve any attorney-client or work-product privilege, and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.250(a). These special interrogatories seek four categories of information: (1) the number of times Botto Law Group administered a trust of equal or greater value than the Foster Trust; (2) the amount of time Botto Law Group spent administering other trusts of equal or greater value than the Foster Trust since January 1, 2010; (3) the total amount billed by Botto Law Group to administer other trusts of equal or greater value than the Foster Trust since January 1, 2010; (4) the identity of all documents that set forth the amount of time Botto Law Group spent administering other trusts of equal or greater value than the Foster Trust since January 1, 2010. In subsequent meet and confers, Petitioners limited the requested time period to 2019 and 2020 and agreed that client information and description of billed activities could be redacted. [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DELIA W. FOSTER 1995 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Delia Foster established the Delia W. Foster 1995 Trust on June 19, 1995, naming herself as trustee. The Trust was allegedly drafted by Respondent Gary Botto and/or Respondent law firm Botto Law Group. Botto was named successor trustee. Foster executed the Twelfth and final amendment to the Trust on March 30, 2015. The amendment was allegedly drafted by Respondent Botto and/or his law firm, and Botto was still named successor trustee. Foster allegedly named Botto her attorney in fact in 2015. Foster died on May 21, 2019. Petitioners are Foster's nephews and heirs at law. On August 27, 2020, they filed Petition for (1) Order Compelling Trust Accounting; (2) Order Compelling Accounting by Attorney in Fact; (3) Breach of Fiduciary Duty; (4) Professional Negligence; (5) Return of Trust Property (Prob. Code section 850); (6) Financial Elder Abuse; (7) Removal of Trustee; (8) Immediate Suspension of Trustee's Powers Pending Removal; and (9) Order Instruction Trustee to Refrain from Using Trust Assets to Defend, naming Botto and Botto Law Group among the Respondents. On February 10, 2022, Petitioners filed Motion to Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto to Provide Further Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One. Petitioners seek further responses to Special Interrogatories No. 6, 7, 47, 48, 49, 50, and Motion to Compel Respondent Botto Law Group to Provide Further Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One. Petitioners seek further responses to Special Interrogatories No. 79, 80, 81, 82. Also on February 10, 2022, Botto filed Motion for Protective Order, Request for Sanctions. The Motions to Compel are scheduled for hearing before the court on July 13, 2022. Botto's Motion for Protective Order is scheduled for hearing before the court on July 20, 2022. The Motion to Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto to Provide Further Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One, as to Special Interrogatories No. 6, 7 is GRANTED. Botto states that he had already produced further responses to Special Interrogatories 6 and 7 in a February 3, 2022 meet and confer letter (Botto Opp. at 5:10-20). Botto is ordered to provide further responses to Special Interrogatories No. 6 and 7 in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.250(a). The Motion to Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto to Provide Further Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One as to Special Interrogatories No. 6, 7, 47, 48, 49, 50 is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Botto is ordered to provide further responses to Special Interrogatories No. 47, 48, 49, 50, limited in time to 2019 and 2020 and with any necessary redactions to preserve any attorney-client or work-product privilege, and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.250(a). These special interrogatories seek four categories of information: (1) the number of times Botto or the law firm administered a trust of equal or greater value than the Foster Trust; (2) the amount of time Botto or the law firm spent administering other trusts of equal or greater value than the Foster Trust since January 1, 2010; (3) the total amount billed by either Botto or the law firm to administer other trusts of equal or greater value than the Foster Trust since January 1, 2010; (4) the identity of all documents that set forth the amount of time Botto or the law firm spent administering other trusts of equal or greater value than the Foster Trust since January 1, 2010. In subsequent meet and confer communications, Petitioners limited the requested time period to 2019 and 2020 and allowed for client information and description of billed activities to be redacted. [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DELIA W. FOSTER 1995 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] To be "discoverable," information must be "relevant to the subject matter" and reasonably calculated to lead to discovery of admissible evidence (CCP section 2017.010). Information is "relevant to the subject matter" if it might "reasonably assist a party in evaluating the case, preparing for trial, or facilitating settlement thereof." (City of Low Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 272, 288.) Petitioners allege that Botto and Botto Law Group overbilled Foster and the Foster Trust intentionally and knowingly. The requested information might reasonably assist Petitioners in evaluating their allegations and - depending on what the information shows - may help facilitate settlement. While the information about administration and service of comparable trust accounts may or may not be admissible, it may lead to discovery of admissible evidence of the alleged breaches of duty and elder abuse. "[E]vidence of other transactions to show motive, intent, knowledge, plan, and absence of mistake" is admissible. (Kirkland v. Sup. Ct. (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 92, 98, quoting Morris Stulsaft Foundation v. Sup. Ct. (1966) 245 Cal.App.2d 409, 422-23.) As discussed in City of Low Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 272, 288, the trial court may limit discovery that otherwise satisfies CCP section 2017.010 if it determines the discovery sought is "[(1)] unreasonably cumulative or duplicative, or is obtainable from some other source that is more convenient, less burdensome, or less expensive; or (2) The selected method of discovery is unduly burdensome or expensive, taking into account the needs of the case, the amount in controversy, and the importance of the issues at stake in the litigation." (Id., quoting CCP section 2019.030(a).) Although the Opposition argues that the discovery requested is obtainable from some other source - Botto has asked to be deposed - and that this method of discovery is unduly burdensome, there is insufficient showing for the court to determine either that deposing Botto would be "more convenient, less burdensome, or less expensive" than special interrogatories that seek billing information and descriptions of billing documents, or how responding to these remaining six disputed Special Interrogatories would be unduly burdensome. Respondent's objections, in addition to not being supported by any specific showing, are also undercut by both (1) Petitioners limiting the requested time period to 2019 and 2020, and (2) Respondents allegedly having already prepared and produced the following documents in response to the Request for Production of Documents: [Part 2 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DELIA W. FOSTER 1995 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Respondent Botto Law Group To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] To be "discoverable," information must be "relevant to the subject matter" and reasonably calculated to lead to discovery of admissible evidence (CCP section 2017.010). Information is "relevant to the subject matter" if it might "reasonably assist a party in evaluating the case, preparing for trial, or facilitating settlement thereof." (City of Low Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 272, 288.) Petitioners allege that Botto and Botto Law Group overbilled Foster and the Foster Trust intentionally and knowingly. The requested information might reasonably assist Petitioners in evaluating their allegations and - depending on what the information shows - may help facilitate settlement. While the information about administration and service of comparable trust accounts may or may not be admissible, it may lead to discovery of admissible evidence of the alleged breaches of duty and elder abuse. "[E]vidence of other transactions to show motive, intent, knowledge, plan, and absence of mistake" is admissible. (Kirkland v. Sup. Ct. (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 92, 98, quoting Morris Stulsaft Foundation v. Sup. Ct. (1966) 245 Cal.App.2d 409, 422-23.) As discussed in City of Low Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (2017) 9 Cal.App.5th 272, 288, the trial court may limit discovery that otherwise satisfies CCP section 2017.010 if it determines the discovery sought is "[(1)] unreasonably cumulative or duplicative, or is obtainable from some other source that is more convenient, less burdensome, or less expensive; or (2) The selected method of discovery is unduly burdensome or expensive, taking into account the needs of the case, the amount in controversy, and the importance of the issues at stake in the litigation." (Id., quoting CCP section 2019.030(a).) Although the Opposition argue that the discovery requested is obtainable from some other source - Botto has asked to be deposed - and that this method of discovery is unduly burdensome, there is insufficient showing for the court to determine either that deposing Botto would be "more convenient, less burdensome, or less expensive" than special interrogatories that seek billing information and descriptions of billing documents, or how responding to these remaining disputed Special Interrogatories would be unduly burdensome. Respondent's objections, in addition to not being supported by any specific showing, are also undercut by both (1) Petitioners limiting the request to 2019 and 2020, and (2) Respondents allegedly having already prepared and produced the following documents in response to the Request for Production of Documents: [A] summary sheet of Respondent's Firm's financial information, spreadsheets with invoice and billing records for the entire Firm identifying time spent on Ms. Foster's matter in comparison to all other matters, individual attorney/paralegal/support staff breakdown of billing rates and hours worked over several years, and a summary sheet with ratios of invoices from the Firm in relation to Ms. Foster's matter. Additionally, as part of the compromise and the Memorandum of Understanding executed after the May 9, 2022 mediation, Respondent's [sic] provided unredacted 2020 billing records for work done for Ms. Foster's Trust, and provided two additional formal accountings that fully report on post-death administration up to the time Respondent resigned from his position as Trustee. BLG Opp. at 9:20-10:3. The supporting declaration to the Opposition states in relevant part: Respondent provided spread sheets, charts, and offered additional disclosures all in good faith, and without waiving legal objections, regarding the number of clients in his practice, the number of active clients in 2019 and 2020, billed hours and recorded hours for 2019 and 2020, personal hours for 2019 and 2020, and total Firm billed amounts for 2019 and 2020. BLG Decl. at par. 6. [Part 2 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DELIA W. FOSTER 1995 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Respondent Gary E. Botto To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] [A] summary sheet of Respondent's Firm's financial information, spreadsheets with invoice and billing records for the entire Firm identifying time spent on Ms. Foster's matter in comparison to all other matters, individual attorney/paralegal/support staff breakdown of billing rates and hours worked over several years, and a summary sheet with ratios of invoices from the Firm in relation to Ms. Foster's matter. Additionally, as part of the compromise and the Memorandum of Understanding executed after the May 9, 2022 mediation, Respondent's [sic] provided unredacted 2020 billing records for work done for Ms. Foster's Trust, and provided two additional formal accountings that fully report on post-death administration up to the time Respondent resigned from his position as Trustee. Botto Opp. at 9:26-10:9. The supporting declaration to the Opposition states in relevant part: Respondent provided spread sheets, charts, and offered additional disclosures all in good faith, and without waiving legal objections, regarding the number of clients in his practice, the number of active clients in 2019 and 2020, billed hours and recorded hours for 2019 and 2020, personal hours for 2019 and 2020, and total Firm billed amounts for 2019 and 2020. Botto Decl. at para. 6. Notwithstanding that Petitioners state they have not received these documents (Reply to Botto at 4:5-9), and that these documents were prepared and produced in response to Petitioners' Request for Production of Documents, there is no showing that responding to the six contested special interrogatories would be unduly burdensome in comparison, or infringe on attorney-client privilege or unspecified privacy rights that have not otherwise already been addressed and protected or waived in the prior production, or that responsive information could not be produced in Code-compliant form. Per the language of CCP section 2023.010 et seq, the court is required to impose a monetary sanction "against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully" opposes a motion to compel further response to interrogatories, unless the court finds imposing the sanction would be unjust or the sanctioned party acted with substantial justification (CCP section 2030.300(d)). The court does not find grounds to support either of the exceptions to the mandatory imposition of monetary sanctions. Respondent Botto is ordered to pay $560 in sanctions to Petitioners within 10 days of service of Notice of Entry of this order. Petitioners shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day prior to the hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DELIA W. FOSTER 1995 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Respondent Botto Law Group To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One

The court does not find grounds to support either of the exceptions to the mandatory imposition of monetary sanctions. Respondent Botto Law Group is ordered to pay $560 in sanctions to Petitioners within 10 days of service of Notice of Entry of this order. Petitioners shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day prior to the hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STANLEY MARLAND COFFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. The statutes and rules of court determine what must be filed and how notice must be given, etc., not the examiner. A will must be lodged by the custodian of the will within 30 days after having knowledge of the death of the testator. [Probate Code Sect. 8200(a).] A proof of service of notice should just be filed, not attached to a supplement. The verification of the first supplement filed on 5/12/22 has not yet been corrected. The proposed executor has not yet addressed whether or not the testator left a separate "Personal Property Memorandum" mentioned in the will at Section 2.01. The dates of birth of two of the decedent's children are inconsistent between the will and the trust
